title: Unable to get version without being root
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

> kind bug

**Description**

I don't think I should need to be root to read the podman version.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
[core@crawford-libvirt-master-0 ~]$ podman --version
ERRO[0000] cannot re-exec process
[core@crawford-libvirt-master-0 ~]$ sudo !!
sudo podman --version
podman version 0.7.3
```

comments.body: Fixed somewhere in 0.8.x - I want to say 0.8.4?

@mheon:

```
$ cat /etc/os-release 
NAME="Red Hat CoreOS"
VERSION="4.0.5470"
ID="rhcos"
ID_LIKE="rhel fedora"
VERSION_ID="4.0"
PRETTY_NAME="Red Hat CoreOS 4.0.5470"
ANSI_COLOR="0;31"
HOME_URL="https://www.redhat.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Red Hat 7"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION="4.0"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Red Hat"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION="4.0"
OSTREE_VERSION=4.0.5470
```
CoreOS should be getting a newer version soon - there were some questions as to where to get Podman from.
Closing as this is fixed in master.
